Base class for [VisualShader](class_visualshader.md#class-visualshader) nodes. Not related to scene nodes.

## Description

Visual shader graphs consist of various nodes. Each node in the graph is a separate object and they are represented as a rectangular boxes with title and a set of properties. Each node also has connection ports that allow to connect it to another nodes and control the flow of the shader.

## Tutorials

- [Using VisualShaders](../tutorials/shaders/visual_shaders.md)

## Enumerations

<a id="enum-visualshadernode-porttype"></a>

enum **PortType**: [🔗](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype)

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-scalar"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_SCALAR** = `0`

Floating-point scalar. Translated to `float` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-scalar-int"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_SCALAR_INT** = `1`

Integer scalar. Translated to `int` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-scalar-uint"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_SCALAR_UINT** = `2`

Unsigned integer scalar. Translated to `uint` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-vector-2d"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_VECTOR_2D** = `3`

2D vector of floating-point values. Translated to `vec2` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-vector-3d"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_VECTOR_3D** = `4`

3D vector of floating-point values. Translated to `vec3` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-vector-4d"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_VECTOR_4D** = `5`

4D vector of floating-point values. Translated to `vec4` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-boolean"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_BOOLEAN** = `6`

Boolean type. Translated to `bool` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-transform"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_TRANSFORM** = `7`

Transform type. Translated to `mat4` type in shader code.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-sampler"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_SAMPLER** = `8`

Sampler type. Translated to reference of sampler uniform in shader code. Can only be used for input ports in non-uniform nodes.

<a id="class-visualshadernode-constant-port-type-max"></a>

[PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) **PORT_TYPE_MAX** = `9`

Represents the size of the [PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype) enum.

---

## Property Descriptions

<a id="class-visualshadernode-property-linked-parent-graph-frame"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **linked_parent_graph_frame** = `-1` [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-property-linked-parent-graph-frame)

-  **set_frame**(value: [int](class_int.md#class-int))
- [int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_frame**()

Represents the index of the frame this node is linked to. If set to `-1` the node is not linked to any frame.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernode-property-output-port-for-preview"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **output_port_for_preview** = `-1` [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-property-output-port-for-preview)

-  **set_output_port_for_preview**(value: [int](class_int.md#class-int))
- [int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_output_port_for_preview**()

Sets the output port index which will be showed for preview. If set to `-1` no port will be open for preview.

---

## Method Descriptions

<a id="class-visualshadernode-method-clear-default-input-values"></a>

 **clear_default_input_values**() [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-method-clear-default-input-values)

Clears the default input ports value.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernode-method-get-default-input-port"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_default_input_port**(type: [PortType](#enum-visualshadernode-porttype))  [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-method-get-default-input-port)

Returns the input port which should be connected by default when this node is created as a result of dragging a connection from an existing node to the empty space on the graph.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernode-method-get-default-input-values"></a>

[Array](class_array.md#class-array) **get_default_input_values**()  [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-method-get-default-input-values)

Returns an [Array](class_array.md#class-array) containing default values for all of the input ports of the node in the form `[index0, value0, index1, value1, ...]`.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernode-method-get-input-port-default-value"></a>

[Variant](class_variant.md#class-variant) **get_input_port_default_value**(port: [int](class_int.md#class-int))  [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-method-get-input-port-default-value)

Returns the default value of the input `port`.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernode-method-remove-input-port-default-value"></a>

 **remove_input_port_default_value**(port: [int](class_int.md#class-int)) [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-method-remove-input-port-default-value)

Removes the default value of the input `port`.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernode-method-set-default-input-values"></a>

 **set_default_input_values**(values: [Array](class_array.md#class-array)) [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-method-set-default-input-values)

Sets the default input ports values using an [Array](class_array.md#class-array) of the form `[index0, value0, index1, value1, ...]`. For example: `[0, Vector3(0, 0, 0), 1, Vector3(0, 0, 0)]`.

---

<a id="class-visualshadernode-method-set-input-port-default-value"></a>

 **set_input_port_default_value**(port: [int](class_int.md#class-int), value: [Variant](class_variant.md#class-variant), prev_value: [Variant](class_variant.md#class-variant) = null) [🔗](#class-visualshadernode-method-set-input-port-default-value)

Sets the default `value` for the selected input `port`.
